def: Blessed; very fine. See صليٰ الله عليه under صليٰ ṣallạ̄.
adj. & s.f. Foolish, silly (woman); — a silly woman.
s.m. A thong, a narrow strip of leather inserted in the seams of shoes, saddles, &c.; a latchet.
more: n. m. Fine leather inserted in the seams of shoes, saddles, etc.
s. m. A thong, narrow slips of leather with which shoes are stitched.

def: s.m. A band of embroidery
subs: s.m. A kind of embroidery consisting of small stars between embroidered bands.

def: (or salmī ) Name of a woman.
s.f. See شالملي śālmali, and سيمل semal.
s.f. The silk-cotton tree, Bombax heptaphyllum ( = semal); — one of the seven Dvīpas or great divisions of the known continent (so called from the above tree, said to grow there).

ety: sāl˚ = Prk. सार(इ), or सारे(इ) = S. शारय(ति), caus. of rt. शृ; — or = Prk. साल(इ), &c., fr. the S. caus. of शल्
def: v.t. To penetrate, pierce, prick; to perforate, bore, drill; — v.n. To prick, smart, ache, pain.

ety: S. सर, or सल+nā = Prk. डओ = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. A rivulet.
more: v. n. See سالنا v. a.
v. n. To pierce, prick, perforate, enter.

def: s.m. A curry of meat, fish, or vegetables.
more: v. a. S. शल्य 1. To bore; drill ( chhednā ). Chārpāī sāl do . 2. ( Met .) To pierce through; hurt; cut to the quick, Saut ke bol kareje meṅ sārat, Aur balmā kī bāt lagat bahut piyārī, De le, deorā! dinā chār gārī ! Wom. song. My co-wife's bitter words do prick me sore, My husband's words I dearly do adore, Yes, brother-in-law! abuse me four days more.
s. m. Meat, or fish, &c. eaten with bread or rice.
1. v. a. To penetrate, to perforate, bore, prick, pierce, drill. 2. v. n. To ache, be in pain, to smart.
s. m. Meat, fish, or vegetables dressed with condiments in various ways, a curry.

def: v.n. To be perforated, be pierced; to pierce, prick, enter (into).
adj. Being in the same world; — s.m. Good or respectable people, gentlefolk.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سُلُوک (sulūk), from Arabic سُلُوك (sulūk). Compare Punjabi سَلُوک (salūk) / ਸਲੂਕ (salūk) and Gujarati સલૂક (salūk).

def: s.f. Being in the same world or sphere (with).
adj. (f. -ā), Playful, sportive, wanton.
adj. Having salt, salted (food), salty; — name of a district.
more: 1. adj. Salted (food). 2. Name of a district.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Salt, salted; seasoned; tasteful, tasty, savoury; beautiful, handsome; interesting, piquant; intelligent; dark-complexioned, nut-brown (complexion); expressive (countenance).
more: adj. S. सलवण 1. Salt; saline. 2. Salted; seasoned; tasty. 1. Miṭhās se jī phir jātā hai, salone se nahīṅ phirtā Sweet is cloying, not so salt. 2. Jab tere peṭ meṅ khuddiyā lāge, mīṭhā aur salauā kyā re! Prov. When hunger gnaws your stomach, or sweet or salt both are alike. 3. Nut-brown ( sāṅvlā ). 4. Beautiful; handsome. Ālī, Siyā-bar kaiso salono! Wom. song.
adj. 1. Salted, salt, seasoned; tasteful. 2. Beautiful, expressive (a countenance).

def: s.m. A horse-doctor, veterinary surgeon; a farrier.
s.m. = sālotrī, q.v. s.m. A horse-doctor, veterinary surgeon; a farrier.
more: n. m. S. शालिहोत्री A horse-doctor; farrier.

def: s.m. A horse-doctor, veterinary surgeon; a farrier.
s.m. Green barley (used as fodder).
more: W. n. m. Fodder of barley.

ety: prob. fr. the Port.; — Sp. zarza; It. salsa
def: s.m. Sarsa or sarsaparilla.
s.m. Name of a fountain in Paradise; — running limpid water, pure, sweet water.
more: s. 1. Name of a fountain in paradise. 2. Running, limpid, sweet water.

ety: salsalā(nā)+āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. इतव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Creeping, crawling; sound as of a snake creeping, rustling; creeping sensation, titillation, irritation (caused by the crawling of an insect on the body), formication; itching; prurience; pricking, piercing; uneasy feeling, doubt; rankling (cf. sursurāhaṭ).
n. f. A creeping, tickling, or itching sensation. Jab zakhm achchhā hone pe ātā hai, to pahle salsalāhaṭ hotī hai .

ety: prob. S. शल् redupl.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi (caus. augment); cf. sarsarānā
def: v.n. To creep, crawl; to feel a creeping or tickling sensation; to itch; to prick, pierce; to be troubled with doubt; to be uneasy; to rankle (in); — to make a rustling sound (as the creeping of a snake), to rustle; — v.t. To cause to simmer or boil, &c., to heat.
more: v. n. 1. ( rauryānā ) To feel a creeping sensation (from an ant, etc.). 2. To tickle; titillate. 3. To itch. 1. Phunsī salsalā rahī hai . 2. Hathelī salsalā rahī hai, kahīṅ se rupayā āëgā .
v. n. 1. To creep (as, a snake), &c. See سرسرانا sarsarānā. 2. (dakh.) To rankle: see کهٿکنا.

def: s.f. contrac. of salsalāhaṭ, q.v.

ety: for A. سلطنة, v.n. fr. سلط; see sult̤ān
def: s.f. Power of dominion, sovereign or ruling power, empire, sovereignty, dominion, rule; — kingdom, empire, realm
more: n. f. 1. An empire; a kingdom. 2. Government rule ( hukūmat , 2). saltanat-i-jamhūrī ; H. panchāëtī rāj . A republic; democracy. saltanat-i-shakhsī ; H. ek kā rāj . Monarchy. saltanat-i-muttahad ; H. milā-rāj , A united kingdom.
s. f. Empire, sovereignty, domimon, reign, kingdom.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سلطنت (saltanat), from Arabic سَلْطَنَة (salṭana).

def: s.m. A kind of fine red cloth, Turkey red.
more: n. m. S. शालु A fine red calico (Turkey red).
s. m. A kind of red cloth (dyed with Morinda).

def: s.m. A child's bib; a pinafore; a couple of pinafores, one worn on the breast, the other on the back, and fastened above and below the shoulders.
more: n. m. A bib; a couple of pinafores, one worn on the breast, the other on the back, fastened above and below the shoulders.
s. m. A childʼs bib, or a couple of pin-afores, one worn on the breast and the other on the back, and fastened above and below the shoulders ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: s.f. The day of full moon in the month Sāwan (July-August), the festival of rakshā-ban-dhan (see rākhī).
more: n. m. S. श्रावणी 1. The day of full moon in Sāvan (July—August). 2. The festival of rakshā-bandhan . See rākhī .
s. f. The full moon in sāwan, at which time the ornament called rāḵẖī is tied round the wrist. See راکهي rāḵẖī.

def: s.m. A little, very little.
s.m. The Trinity
s.m. = P سالوسي sālūsī, s.f. Trick, fraud, deceit, subterfuge, hypocrisy.
s.f. = P سالوس sālus, s.m. Trick, fraud, deceit, subterfuge, hypocrisy.
(v.n. fr. سلو 'to be content,' &c.), s.m.(?) A quail; — honey.
s.f. The cost of having (a thing) perforated or pierced, &c.
v.t. To cause to be perforated or pierced, &c., to have or get (a thing) pierced, &c.
more: n. f. The cost of perforating a bed-frame.

def: adj. See s.v. sāl, 'year.' s.m. The saul tree, Shorea robusta, and its wood; — the plane-tree; — a wall surrounding a building, a wall (in general); enclosure, court; — a kind of fish, Ophiocephalus wrahl; — the gilt-head fish, Sparus spilotus
subs: s.f. or sāl-pāṇi, s.f. lit. 'Having sāl leaves'; the shrub Hedysarum gangeticum
s.m. A flower of the sāl-tree; — Hibiscus mutabilis
s.m. The resinous juice of the sāl-tree.

ety: for A. سلوة, inf. n. of سلو 'to be content,' &c.
def: s.f. Patient endurance, content, comfort, solace, cheerfulness, happiness, tranquillity.
more: (Heb. Chald. from سلا Heb. To be tranquil or contented) s. m. Content, cheerfulness, felicity.

def: s.m. = شليه śalya, q.v.

s.m. A dart, javelin, spear, pike; an arrow; an iron crow; a stake, rod, spike, splinter, thorn; a pin, peg; — a porcupine; a hedgehog; — extraction of splinters or extraneous substances in surgery; any extraneous substance lodged in the body (as an arrow, or a splinter, &c.); a bone; the thorny shrub Vangueria spinosa; — a fence, a boundary; — sin, crime; poison; difficulty, embarrassment, distress; defamation, abuse; — name of the king of Madra (maternal uncle of the Pāṇdav princes); name of a son of Vipraćitti
subs: s.m. That branch of surgery which treats of the removal of extraneous substances lodged in the body

(˚ya+ud˚), s.m. The extraction of extraneous substances (as arrows, splinters, thorns, &c.) from the body; the ceremony of cleansing the site of a new house from bones or other unclean substances; the ceremony of cleansing or purifying a new house.
ex: ہمارے کشور کے لیے زیادہ سے زیادہ راجہ شلیہ جیسے مرد ہونے چاہئیں ہے
hamāre kiśvar ke liye zyāda se zyāda rāja śalya jaise mard hone cāh'ī̃ he
let there be many men like king Shalya for our fatherland
کس کی فرہ ہمارے شہنشاہ شلیہ سے بہترین ہے
kis kī farrah hamāre śehanśāh śalya se behtarīn hai?
whose glory is greater than our king of kings, Shalya?
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit शल्य (śalya, “spear; javelin; arrow”). Distantly related to Ancient Greek κῆλον (kêlon, “shafts (of arrows)”), Old Prussian kelian (“spear”), Old Norse hali, Albanian thel.

ety: Pārsī śãm; Pehl. śām; Zend śāma, rt. śam = S. सम्
def: s.m. Name of a celebrated hero in Persian romance (father to Zāl, and grandfather to Rustam); — a swelling (e.g. sar-sām, 'swelling in the head').
more: n. prop. Name of the father of Zāl and grandfather of Rustam.
s. f. See S̱. سامي sāmī.

def: s.f. A ferule ( = sāmī).
s.m. = syām; — 2˚, s.f. = shām, qq.v. adj. Black; dark blue; dark brown, swarthy, nut-brown; — s.m. An epithet of Krishṇ; — the Indian cuckoo (syn. koʼil or koyal)
s.m. = شم śam, q.v. s.m. Name of a celebrated hero in Persian romance (father to Zāl, and grandfather to Rustam); — a swelling (e.g. sar-sām, 'swelling in the head').
insep. prep. or prefix = sam, q.v. s.m. Name of a celebrated hero in Persian romance (father to Zāl, and grandfather to Rustam); — a swelling (e.g. sar-sām, 'swelling in the head').
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سن (sinn), from Arabic سِنّ (sinn).

def: adj. Black, dark ( = syām); — s.m. A name of Krishṇ
subs: s.m. 'Dark and beautiful'; an epithet of Krishṇ; a proper name.

def: s.f. The iron head of a pestle, &c.; ferule (of a stick, &c.), mounting; a socket; (in Mech.) a carrier
subs: (-meṅ), To tip or shoe with iron, &c.; to get mounted; to put on a ferule, &c.

def: s.m. Appeasing; reconciling; conciliation; reconciliation.
adv. Happily, fortunately, auspiciously.
s.m. Hearing, listening to; — music, &c. ( = samāʻ); — the sense of hearing; — the organ of hearing, the ear
more: n. m. 1. Hearing. 2. Music. sama-kharāshī , Dazing one's brains by talking overmuch to him. Āp kī bahut sama-kharāshī huī, muāf kījegā!
subs: s.f. Grating or jarring on the ear; dazing the brains (by excessive talk, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَمْع (sam'), from Arabic سَمْع (samʕ).

def: insep. prep. or prefix (opp. to vi, and, like Gr. σύν, Lat. con, expressing) With, together with, along with, together: (it sometimes intensifies the idea contained in the simple root, and may often be translated by) much, greatly, very, wholly, thoroughly, perfectly, well, beautifully, &c.
more: n. m. Poison; venom ( bis ). sammi'yat , n. m. Poisonous property.

Pref.; H. sā, sa , Suf. 1. Alike; similar. 2. An inseparable prep, signifying together, altogether with, together with. 3. In compos . = Eng. con-, col-, com-, cor-), Opp. of वि It generally implies conjunction, fullness, perfection, etc.

1. ( सम ) adj. Like, alike: all, entire. 2. ( सम् or सं ) an inseparable prep. signifying, Together, altogether, with, together with (in comp. con-, col-, com-): it is the reverse of वि , and generally implies conjunction, fulness, perfection, &c.

def: s.f. Apparatus, &c. ( = sāmagrī, and sāmān, qq.v.); — provisions, food of various kinds.
more: s. f. Apparatus, provisions, furniture, articles. See سامگري sāmagrī.

def: s.m. Time, season; season of plenty, season of greatest abundance or prevalence; plenty, abundance; opportunity; — state, condition; concord, harmony, unison; — scene, landscape, view
more: n. m. S. समय 1. Time; season. 1. Rājā ke darbār meṅ jaiye samayo pāë, Sāīṅ, tahāṅ na baiṭhye, jahāṅ kou de uṭhāë! Jahāṅ koī dei uṭhāë bol an-bole rahiye! Haṅsye, nāh hirāë, bāt pūchhe teṅ kahiye! Kaheṅ Girdhar Kabirāë, samaë soṅ kīje kājā! Atī ātur nahīṅ hoë, bahur ankhe hai rājā . Girdhar. When chance shall serve that you a royal court essay, Sit not, where one may say 'go lower down, I pray!' And wheresoe'er you 're placed keep silence nor aught say, Nor laugh, nor speak, but when you 're spoken to you may, Says Girdhar, 'let each act upon occasion stay,' 'Quick be not lest the king to you rebuke convey!' 2. Kaheṅ Girdhar, Kabirāë, tanik nahīṅ kīnā ros'ā, Same paṛe kī bāt, bilār ko sīkh det hai mūsā . Girdhar Kunḍalya. The Poet Girdhar says, 'take not, I pray, my words amiss, As times shall go the mouse with counsel shall the cat dismiss.' 3. Samā kare, nar kyā kare, sameṅ sameṅ kī bāt…
s. m. 1. Time, season. 2. Plenty, abundance. 3. State, condition. 4. Concord, harmony, unison. سما بندهنا samā bandhnā, v. n. To be in concert.
(Heb. ) s. m. 1. The heavens, the sky, the firmament. 2. A canopy. 3. Altitude.
subs: (-kā), To make a picture or representation (of), to represent; — to act, sing, or play in concert, to produce harmony; to be in concert or harmony
v.n. To find or get an opportunity.

def: s.f. A year.

ety: v.n. fr. سمو 'to be or become high,' &c.
def: s.m. The heavens, sky, firmament; a canopy; height, altitude, meridian, highest or uppermost part (of anything), culminating point, prime, spring.

s.m. Hearing, listening to; listening to music or singing; the sense of hearing; — singing, song, music (instrumental or vocal); ecstasy occasioned by hearing singing or music (particularly in Derwishes when hearing hymns).
more: n. m. سمع heard. 1. ( samāٴat ) Hearing. 2. Music; singing. 3. Religious ecstacy or frenzy. ( Sūfīs ).
(from سمع Heb. To hear) s. m. 1. Hearing, listening. 2. The sense of hearing. 3. Singing, a song. 4. Ecstacy occasioned by hearing singing, particularly in Dervises when hearing hymns.

def: s.m. Blackness, impurity, mouldiness, &c.; — a kind of edible grain, Panicum frumentaceum or P. colonum.
more: s. m. 1. ( श्यामा ) The little bird generally called Shāma. 2. ( श्यामाक ) Name of a grain. (Panicum colonum or frumentaceum).

def: s.f. (m. ?) A beautiful Indian song-bird, the Indian nightingale, Turdus macrourus.
s.m. A hymn, a particular kind of sacred text or verse that is to be chanted

vulg. sām-bedī, s.m. A Brāhmaṇ who studies the Sāma-veda.

def: adj. Even, level, flat, plain, smooth; same, equal, like, similar; all, whole, entire, full, complete; — s.m. A level plain; (in Geom.) a mean proportional segment; — (in music) measuring time; time; a tone
subs: (˚ma+āć˚), adj. & s.m. Equal or similar in practice or in virtuous conduct; — equal or similar conduct; upright or virtuous conduct, proper practice
adj. & s.m. Of like nature or property; — equality, similarity, equability, sameness; equilibrium; goodness
s.f. Even or level ground; a race-course
adj. & s.m. Having three sides equal; — an equilateral triangle

adj. & adv. Of equal weight, equiponderant; equal, even, matching, rivaling, of the same grade, of equal ability, of equal value or merit; duly balanced, equable; evenly or harmoniously proportioned (as a temperament or constitution, &c.); — evenly, equally on both sides (as a weight pressing, &c.)
adj. (f. -ā), Of the same caste or class, of the same origin or kind, homogeneous
adj. & s.m. Equally four-sided; — an equilateral tetragon or rhombus
vulg. sam-darsī, adj. & s.m. Viewing or regarding equally, looking at both sides impartially, impartial; — an impartial person
adj. & s.m. Sharing (another's) pain or grief, feeling for another's woe, sympathizing (with); — a sympathetic person, sympathizer
adj. Having the same grief and joy, sympathizing in sorrow and joy
adj. Plane, level, level with the ground
adj. (f. -ā), Belonging to the same country or locality
adj. Of the same form or figure; — s.m. Conformity; uniformity
s.m. The horizon; the north or south point of the horizon; a pole of the prime vertical
s.m. Equality in wealth; — adj. (f. -ā), Possessing equal wealth
sam-kālin, or sam-kālīn, adj. Of or belonging to the same time or period, contemporary, contemporaneous, coeval
s.f. A suitable maiden, a girl fit to be married
adj. & s.m. Rectangular, right-angled; — a right angle
s.m. A species of metre in which all the four feet are equal

def: s.m. Quiet, tranquillity, rest, calm, peace; quietism, quiet of mind; restraint of the organs of sense; indifference to objects of sense; the absence of passion; indifference, stoicism; abstract meditation on Brahmā; emancipation from all the illusions of existence, final happiness; — allayment (of pain), alleviation or cure (of disease), convalescence; — the hand; — imprecation, malediction, abuse.
s.m. sāmak, and sāṅwā, qq.v.
s.f. corr. of شياما śyāmā, q.v. s.m. An epithet of Krishṇ; the Indian nightingale (see shāmā).
adj. Roomy, capacious, fit to hold; — s.m. A vessel in which fire is kept.

ety: for A. سماعة, inf. n. of سمع 'to hear'
def: s.f. Hearing, the sense of hearing; hearing or taking evidence (in a law-suit, &c.), cognizance
more: n. f. 1. (H. sarvan ) The sense of hearing. 2. (H. sunvāī ) Hearing or trial (of a suit); cognizance. Samāٴat meṅ farq ā gayā hai . samāٴat k. , v. a. To hear or try (a suit); to entertain or take cognizance of (a complaint, etc.). samāٴat ke qābil, qābil-i-samāٴat , adj. Admissible; cognizable (a suit). samāٴat-i-muqaddamah . The hearing of a case or suit. ikhtiyār-i-samāٴat , The power of trying a case; jurisdiction. hadd-i-samāٴat . Statute of limitation. hadd-i-samāٴat se bāhar . Out of the statute of limitation.
subs: (-kī), To hear, to take evidence (in a suit), to try (a case), to entertain or take cognizance (of a complaint)
adj. Worthy or deserving of a hearing, admissible, cognizable (a suit).

vulg. sām-bedī, s.m. A Brāhmaṇ who studies the Sāma-veda.

def: s.m. (in (rhetoric) Mentioning together; proximity (to), or association (with a word the meaning of which is well understood); bringing together, association, company.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. sama or sam. s.m. A hymn, a particular kind of sacred text or verse that is to be chanted
more: n. m. See حال (1, 3, 4.) 1. State. 2. Tidings ( khabar , 1). Yahāṅ ke samāchār bhale haiṅ . All well here. 3. The advice of a bill. samāchār kahnā , v. a. To report; give tidings of one.
s. m. News, tidings, information, intelligence, advice, account of circumstances or health, &c.
subs: vulg. sām-bed, s.m. Name of one of the three principal Vedas (the prayers of which are composed in metre, and are always sung or chanted)
vulg. sām-bedī, s.m. A Brāhmaṇ who studies the Sāma-veda.

def: s.m. Proceeding, going; performance, practice, conduct, behaviour; usage, way; proper practice or behaviour; state, condition, circumstances, doings, news, report, information, tidings, intelligence, message, advice (of a bill), account of circumstances, health; an occurrence; tradition
subs: (-kā), To give news or tidings (of), to report
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit समाचार (samācāra, “proceeding, conduct, news, information”).

ety: v.n. fr. صمد 'to repair, or have recourse (to
def: s.m. A lord, chief man, master, head (of a family, &c.); the Most High, the Eternal, God; — adj. High, sublime; perpetual, eternal.
more: 1. s. m. Lord, master. 2. adj. Eternal, high; the eternal.

def: s.m. corr. of sam-vād, q.v. s.m. Conversation, discourse; history, story; information, news; communication (of news or intelligence).
more: n. f. S. सम्बाद Message; tidings. Bhoj na bhāt, naihar kā samād . Prov. Nor bread nor rice, at my parent's house a feast!

def: adj. Eternal; divine ( = ṣamadī; for the -ānī, which is borrowed from the Aramaic, cf. jismānī, rūḥānī, &c.).
s.m. Great respect, reverence, veneration.
adj. See s.v. sama or sam. s.m. A hymn, a particular kind of sacred text or verse that is to be chanted
subs: vulg. sām-bed, s.m. Name of one of the three principal Vedas (the prayers of which are composed in metre, and are always sung or chanted)
vulg. sām-bedī, s.m. A Brāhmaṇ who studies the Sāma-veda.

def: s.f. Profound or abstract meditation, restraining the senses and confining the mind to contemplation (especially on the true nature of spirit, &c.); intense absorption or contemplation; silence, devotion; abstracted or absorbed state; a religious vow or self-imposed restraint; self-immolation (of a sanyāsī) by drowning or by burying himself alive; an exercise of austerity (among jogīs) whereby they are supposed to acquire the power of suspending during pleasure the connexion between soul and body; — the tomb of a sanyāsī (who has been buried alive); a tomb over the relics of a dead body, or a little edifice, to contain the tulsī plant, which is erected over the burial place of a sanyāsī
more: s. f. 1. Tomb of a Jogī, particularly where Hindūs, from religious motives, submit to be buried alive. 2. An exercise of austerity among Jogīs, whereby they are supposed to acquire the power of suspending during their pleasure the connexion between their soul and body.
subs: samādh lenā, To suspend (one's) breath for a fabulous period of time; to submit to be buried alive.

def: n. f. S. ममाधि 1. The tomb of a Sanyāsī Jogī , originally one who submitted to be buried alive. 2. An exercise among Jogīs whereby they are believed to acquire the power of suspending at pleasure the union of soul and body. 3. A tomb over the relies of a dead body. samādh lagānā yā lenā, tālī lagānā , v. a. 1. To suspend one's breath for fabulous periods. 2. To submit to be buried alive.
s.m. The mutual embracing of two samdhīs.

s.m. Fixing the mind in abstract contemplation (as on the true nature of spirit), religious abstraction (as practised by Hindūs), restraining the senses by confining the mind to contemplation, profound absorption or contemplation, religious meditation; intentness, eagerness; — promising; declaring, declaration; adjustment, decision, determination; the act of satisfying, propitiating; pacification; comfort, consolation, solace.

def: n. f. S. सम + बुध 1. See budh , 1, 2. 1. Apnī apnī samajh kā pher hai .Prov.Saying. Many men of many minds. ( Sot homines, tot sententiœ ). 2. Tumhārī samajh par to patthar paṛ gaë . Thoṛī thoṛī mohe samajh jo āī, ek jā thairī morī sagāī, Āvan lāge bāman, nāī, koī le rupaiyā, koī le dhelīt Girls' song. 2. To get sense; return to one's senses. samajh būjh . Saying. Knowledge is death. (Ignorance is bliss). samajh meṅ ānā , v. n. To understand; comprehend. achchhī samajh . A good understanding. ulṭī samajh . A crooked understanding.

def: v.t. & v.n. To perceive, know, understand, comprehend, apprehend; to learn; — to think, consider, conceive, suppose, deem, imagine, fancy; to think highly of, to consider as good or excellent or remarkable; — to come to an understanding (with, -se), to look (to one, for explanation, or payment, &c.), to settle accounts (with, either fig. or lit.), to be even (with)
more: v. n. 1. See būjhnā , 1, 3. 1. Huā so huā, ab to samjho . 2. Kuchh tum samjhe, kuchh ham samjhe . Prov. You think one way, and I another. ☞ A foot passenger, carrying some treasure, asked a horseman to relieve him of his load as far as the next stage. The horseman declined to burden himself, and went on. Soon after the horseman regretted that he had missed such a rare opportunity of making off with so much money, while the foot pad, on his part, congratulated himself on the other's refusal, but for which he might have lost his money. The two met again, and, on the horseman offering to take charge of the treasure the other replied in the words of the Proverb. 2. To look to one (for payment, etc.). Chunarī kī rangāī more pīyā se samajh lījo . Wom. 3. To retaliate; to be even with. samajh-ke , adv. Knowingly; deliberately. samajh-ke k. , v. a. To act intelligently; to exercise discretion. b…
(see سمجهہ samajh ) v. a. To understand, comprehend, suppose, think, conceive, consider, deem, fancy, perceive, apprehend, know, come to an understanding (with).
subs: (-ko), and samajh-lenā, v.t. intens. of and = samajhnā.

ety: samā˚ = samāw˚ = Prk. समाव(इ or सम्+आप्
def: v.n. To be contained or held (in, -meṅ), to go or get (in or into), to enter (in or into), to fit (in); to take up room fill or occupy space.
more: v. n. सं + मा 1. To be contained in; to fit; go into. Naṭ chaṛhe, bāṅs baṛhe; naṭ utre ghaṭ jāë, E sakhī, maiṅ to se pūckhūṅ naṭ meṅ bāṅs samāë? Riddle. Ans. Makṛī. Spider. The clown! the pole! It grows and shrinks as he goes up and down: Can you explain it dear? Methinks it's packed inside the clown. 2. Jahāṅ jiske sīng samāëṅ vahāṅ chale jāo . Where'er your horn fits in there go! (Live where you can). 3. Kājal ḍālūṅ kirkirā, aur surmā dīya na jāë, In nainan meṅ pī base, dūjā kaun samāë? Wom. Lamp-black doth prick my eyes, for eye-paint there's no space, My love dwells in my eyes, for aught else there's no place. 4. Zamīn phat jāë to maiṅ samā jāoṅ! Would the earth but open that I the gap might fill! 2. To fill, or occupy space. 1. Ek samānā sakal meṅ, sakal samāṅā tāhī . God dwelleth in all things and all things dwell in Him. 2. Sūrat mere mitr kī man meṅ rahī samāë, Joṅ meṅhdī ke pāt …
To be contained in, to go into. See امانا amānā.

more: n. m. 1. The mutual relationship between two parties whose children are married to each other. 2. The respective parental homes of the wedded pair. Buā, samdhiyāne ke ṭeṛhe māmle hote haiṅ, kyoṅ-kar khālī hāth bhej dūṅ? Mah. Wom. My dear! the relations between the paternal houses of the wedded pair are very delicate, how can I send one there empty-handed?
s. m. The family of a childʼs father-in-law.

def: s. (dakh.) Sea: see سمدر samudr.
adj. & s.m. Hearing; — hearer, listener.
more: n. m. سمع heard. A hearer; listener.
(part. act. of سمع Heb. and Ch. To hear) s. m. A hearer. (Plur. سامعين).

def: n. m. S. समय 1. Time; period. 2. Fit or proper time. Samaë nā bāram-bār . Prov. Opportunities neglected never return. 3. Season ( rut ). 1. Kā barkhā jo krikhī sukhānī, Same chūk, phir kā pachhtānī? Bhoj. Prov. What if it rains? all dried up now the field, The season past, naught vain regrets can yield. 2. Same same kī bāt, bāj par jhapṭe bagulā . Girdhar. Each age and season hath its lay, upon the hawk the crane doth prey! 3. Tāhe samaë paṭ ḍhūṅḍan nikisīṅ, kahīṅ nahīṅ dishṭ paṛo rī, Tin meṅ ek sakhī uṭh bolī, 'dekh kadam kī orī, Chīr sab jāë dharo rī!' Sāṅore ko charitr suno rī! Holī. Sūr Dās. The maids from out the water come no where their clothes could see, When one cried out 'See there they are upon the Kadam tree!' To the nut-brown god Kanhaiya's gambols come and listen ye! 4. Noh se chhīl chhīl dāl khilāī, pānī bhar bhar piyālī, Jab paṛhne kī samaë jo āī uṛ gayā lāl, pinjrā bhayā khālī . The soul. Bhajan . Kabir. With pulse blanched daily fed, cups full of water too supplied, Just as 't was time to learn his cage he empty left and fled. same-dastkhat . A signature to an account in acknowledgment of its correctness. same same , adv. Periodically; regularly recurring. Same same sundar sabhī, rūp ku-rūp na koë . Prov. All handsome in their place, fitness the only grace.

def: v.t. To accomplish, fulfil, finish, dispose of; to roll together, roll up, fold up, wrap up; to gather up or together, to scrape or rake together; to crumple up; to constringe, to contract, to cause to shrink or shrivel; to compress; to brace up; to condense (in writing, &c.); to collect together, to accumulate, amass, hoard
more: (caus. of سمٿنا simaṭnā, q. v.) v. a. 1. To collect together, amass. 2. To constringe, contract, compress, brace, to cause to shrivel or shrink.

def: v. a. 1. To collect ( ikhaṭṭā k . 1, 2, 4). 2. To scrape together; rake up. 3. To condense (in writing). 4. To contract; constringe. Dāman sameṭ-ke chalo! 5. To finish; accomplish. Sārā kām sameṭ dīyā. sameṭā-sameṭī , n. f. Accumulating (money).
